external.description: Revenge porn, also known as non-consensual pornography or non-consensual intimate image distribution (often abbreviated as NCII or image-based sexual abuse / IBSA), refers to the sharing, distribution, or publication of sexually explicit images or videos of a person without their consent.  The term "revenge porn" originated from cases where an ex-partner shares intimate photos or videos (often originally sent consensually during a relationship) to humiliate, punish, or get revenge after a breakup. However, the behavior extends far beyond "revenge" motives. Perpetrators may act out of harassment, blackmail, control, financial gain, notoriety, or other reasons. Images can also be obtained without any initial consent (e.g., through hidden cameras, hacking, upskirting, deepfakes, or digital manipulation).  Key Elements Typically Involved Sexually explicit content — Images or videos showing nudity, intimate body parts (genitals, anus, buttocks, female breasts with nipple exposed), or sexual acts. Lack of consent for distribution — The person depicted did not agree (or withdrew agreement) to the sharing/public posting. Identifiability — The victim is recognizable from the image itself or associated information. Intent or effect — Many legal definitions require intent to harass, humiliate, intimidate, cause emotional distress, or harm (though some laws focus more on the non-consensual act itself).  Important Distinctions in Terminology Many experts, advocacy groups (e.g., Cyber Civil Rights Initiative), and legal frameworks now prefer terms like non-consensual intimate image abuse or image-based sexual abuse over "revenge porn". The word "revenge" can minimize the abuse by implying the victim's behavior "provoked" it or by suggesting it's only about romantic retaliation, when in reality the motive varies and the harm is the same regardless.  This form of abuse is a serious violation of privacy and sexual autonomy. It often causes significant emotional distress, reputational damage, job loss, relationship issues, and in extreme cases, has been linked to self-harm or suicide.  Legal Status (Focusing on the United States) All 50 states now have criminal laws prohibiting the non-consensual distribution of intimate images (as of 2025, with South Carolina being the last to pass such legislation). There is also a federal civil cause of action under the Violence Against Women Act (VAWA) reauthorization (2022), allowing victims to sue for damages. Additional federal protections exist under newer laws like the Take It Down Act (which criminalizes certain publications/threats and requires platforms to remove content upon request).  If this is something you're dealing with personally or professionally (e.g., in psychology, victim support, or cyber abuse research), resources like the Cyber Civil Rights Initiative (cybercivilrights.org), StopNCII.org, or victim services organizations can provide removal assistance, legal referrals, and support.
external.title: What is Revenge Porn?

external.description: Sextortion is a form of online blackmail (a portmanteau of "sex" and "extortion") in which a perpetrator threatens to distribute, share, or expose sexually explicit images, videos, or other intimate/compromising material of a victim unless the victim complies with specific demands.  These demands typically include: Sending additional nude or sexually explicit images/videos Performing sexual acts (often on camera) Paying money, cryptocurrency, or gift cards  The perpetrator usually obtains the initial compromising material through: Deception (posing as a peer, romantic interest, or trustworthy person on social media, dating apps, gaming platforms, or video chats) Coercion or manipulation into sharing content Hacking, malware, or accessing existing private material (less common in many reported cases) Tricking victims during live interactions (e.g., using fake webcam feeds)  There are two primary variants:  Traditional sextortion — The offender seeks more sexual content for their own gratification, threatening to release existing material publicly or to the victim's contacts. Financially motivated sextortion (increasingly common) — The primary goal is money, often targeting teens and young adults with demands for payments to prevent release of the images.  Sextortion disproportionately affects minors and young people, though adults can also be victims. Authorities like the FBI and National Center for Missing & Exploited Children (NCMEC) describe it as a serious crime involving coercion and child sexual exploitation when minors are involved. It has seen explosive growth in recent years, driven by anonymous online platforms, with reports showing sharp increases (e.g., financial sextortion reports to NCMEC roughly doubled in parts of 2025 compared to prior periods, and cases continue rising alarmingly into 2026 in various regions).  Victims often experience severe psychological distress, shame, anxiety, and in tragic cases, self-harm or suicide.  Key prevention points include never sharing intimate images online, being cautious with unknown contacts, and immediately stopping communication and reporting if threatened (without paying, as that often leads to more demands).  If this relates to a specific concern or incident, resources like the FBI (tips.fbi.gov or 1-800-CALL-FBI), NCMEC CyberTipline (cybertipline.org), or local law enforcement can provide direct help.
external.title: What is Sextortion?

record.text:
And yes there are other issues with AI safety, brainrot, hallucinations etc.

I think we'll solve most of these through either technical or cultural development over time.

But wouldn't we be more likely to build be actual benefit and safety if we took profit out for the equation?
Smaller, inference data centres which host your own chatgpt and personal data could be community funded and hosted in the existing repository and local interface to our cultural world - the library.

I want AI to succeed and be good. But I want the foundational models to be owned by the commons. They should belong to humanity, not oligarchs.

I want ai training data centres to be built as strategic national resources in line with green energy policy and development.

->Just Security | More on "Iran attack international law legitimacy" at BigEarthData.ai | #Iran

external.description: A momentous gap has emerged between the broad consensus among international lawyers regarding the illegality of Operation Epic Fury (or Roaring Lion) launched by the United States and Israel against Iran on Feb. 28, 2026, and the mild or even supportive reactions afforded to the operation by many States. While part of the gap can be explained by notions of political affinity to the United States and hostility toward Iran, and by reference to Iran’s own unlawful reaction to the attack (under both jus ad bellum and jus in bello), we believe this gap may also be indicative of a more fundamental crisis afflicting the international rule of law (or the rules-based international order (RBIO)): a growing perception among policymakers that international law no longer offers a workable framework of norms and institutions for addressing the most serious problems of international peace and security. Many of us have been there: in the driver’s seat on a tricky hill start, when the engine cuts out and the car judders to a halt. Since the EU-UK summit last May – the event that was supposed to map a new and dynamic way forward – stalling is fast becoming a real risk for Keir Starmer’s most crucial long-term policy area: improving Britain’s relationship with the EU.

Today’s report by MPs on parliament’s foreign affairs committee rightly warns that despite the hugely welcome – and essential – progress in relations with our closest allies and neighbours, Starmer’s project is “suffering from a lack of direction, definition and drive”. If Labour is to deliver a growing economy, improve living standards for every UK resident and tackle the very real threat of Reform UK, getting the EU reset back into gear is a matter of urgency.

The part that technology can't really do is that final risk assessment for considering a target.

The first use I saw was basically using some kind of system to scour imagery for specific things - high value targets like radar and electronic warfare systems - and create a target list which analysts then could use as their start point to move along the process.

AI-type applications for targeting aren't really new, intelligence analysts used to spend hours poring over satellite imagery to do this and it can now largely be automated, but it definitely isn't a good idea to remove all human influence on those decisions, especially with collateral damage risk.

In a recent report from Socket, a compromised release of the Aqua Trivy VS Code extension on OpenVSX (v1.8.12 and v1.8.13) contained unauthorized code that injected prompts targeting local AI coding agents such as Copilot, Claude, and Codex.

The prompts attempted to trigger coding agents in YOLO mode to inspect the system and expose sensitive data.

@udgover added the prompts to PromptIntel for tracking and analysis! 😎

https://promptintel.novahunting.ai/feed

En el pueblito tenemos un vecino alemán que le pone empeño a comunicarse en castellano y tengo pruebas.
Quería ver los goya y le pasé el enlace de RTVE.
En este mail cuenta muchas cosas interesantes sobre como nos posicionamos españoles y alemanes ante la guerra y por qué, por eso lo comparto.

images.alt:
Texto de un email que me ha alegrado la mañana en el curro:
Buenos días, querida...
Hice clic en el enlace "Goya internetverweis" (aquí quiere decir que pincho en el enlace que le pase de RTVE play para que viera los goya en directo) y luego en el enlace "kommentarsendung Internetverweis" (aquí supongo que se refiere a que pincho en el siguiente enlace para poder verlo), por lo que me perdí el comienzo.
Pero hay una grabación.
Me gusta mucho que los españoles tengáis tanta confianza en vosotros mismos como para celebrar vuestra propia "entrega de los Óscar". Cuando vi las insignias gratuitas, primero pensé que se trataba de entradas gratuitas, hasta que vi en un primer plano que se trataba de una declaración política. Me gustó mucho que los españoles protestarán discretamente contra la guerra ilegal en este programa de televisión. En Alemania siempre nos inculcan un complejo de culpa. Por supuesto, me alegró especialmente que dos películas que también se estrenaron en nuestros cines ganarán Goyas. 
Te deseo un buen día de trabajo. Saludos cordiales. 
Y firma con su nombre.

📢【Bless】bekommt eine Anime-Adaption! ✨️

📅 Geplanter Start 》2027
✨ Genre 》Slice of Life 
🎬 Studio 》A-1 Pictures
▶ Teaser 》
youtu.be/ECI_iIX7LU0?...

#ブレス #BLESS #園山ゆきの #アニメ #Anime #AnimeNews

images.alt:
Mit einem Teaser Trailer wurde heute bekannt geben, dass der Slice of Life Manga »Bless« von Yukino Sonoyama eine Anime-Adaption erhält. Der Anime startet im Laufe des nächsten Jahres 2027 im japanischen TV. Zudem stellte man die Mitwirkenden an der Produktion offiziell vor.

»Bless« erscheint seit Januar 2022 im Shounen Magazine Edge von Kodansha und umfasst bislang 8 Bände. Band 9 erscheint am 9. März in Japan. Eine englische Übersetzung gibt es bei Kodansha USA, aktuell sind dort 7 Bände verfügbar.

»Bless« entsteht bei A-1 Pictures (Kaguya-sama: Love is War) unter der Regie von Shōtarō Kitamura (Makeine: Too Many Losing Heroines!), die Series Composition und das Skript übernimmt Kamiza Fukurō (Deaimon), das Character Design stammt von Honoka Yokoyama (Oshi no Ko), und die Musik komponiert Ryō Yoshimata (Koi wa Ameagari no You ni).

📜 Beschreibung 》
In einer lauten Schulklasse wird der 15-jährige Aia Udagawa plötzlich als Model für einen Wettbewerb beim Aoi Festival ausgewählt. Dabei kann er seine Augen kaum von Jun Sumisaki lassen, einem großen, unbeholfenen Mädchen, das sich für ihre Sommersprossen und ihre Größe schämt. Eigentlich sollten die beiden als Model und Stylist zusammenarbeiten, doch Udagawa hat seinen Traum, Makeup Artist zu werden, längst aufgegeben. Als Sumisaki vorschlägt, die Rollen praktisch umzudrehen, bekommen beide eine Chance, über sich hinauszuwachsen.

🎶 OP 》„ATTITUDE“ von aespa
🎵 ED 》„KILL SHOT“ von RIIZE

Auf der offiziellen Website der Anime-Umsetzung stellten die Verantwortlichen die Interpreten der Theme-Songs offiziell vor. Der Opening-Song »ATTITUDE« wird von aespa gesungen, während der Ending-Song »KILL SHOT« von RIIZE stammt. Der Anime startet am 11. April im japanischen TV.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Der legendäre Auftragskiller Juuzou Oogami kennt kein Scheitern. Doch nach einem Einsatz wird er von einer mysteriösen Wespe gestochen und wacht als 13 jähriger Junge wieder auf. Noch bevor er begreift, was passiert ist, bekommt er den nächsten Auftrag: Er soll in dieser Gestalt eine Mittelschule infiltrieren. Zwischen chaotischem Schulalltag, auffälligen Mitschülern und neuen Gefahren stellt sich nur eine Frage: Schafft Juuzou es, wieder zu seinem alten Körper zurückzukehren, oder endet sein zweites Leben, bevor es richtig beginnt?
